article_title: OpenAI's Robotics Hardware Lead Resigns Over Pentagon Deal, Citing Rushed Guardrails on Surveillance and Autonomous Weapons

content_preview.title: OpenAI's Robotics Hardware Lead Resigns Over Pentagon Deal, Citing Rushed Guardrails on Surveillance and Autonomous Weapons
content_preview.summary: Caitlin Kalinowski left OpenAI after the company signed a classified Pentagon agreement without sufficient deliberation on safeguards against domestic surveillance and lethal autonomy.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Caitlin Kalinowski, the hardware executive who led OpenAI's robotics team, resigned on March 7 in protest of the company's agreement to deploy its AI models on a classified Department of Defense network, according to [NPR](https://www.npr.org/2026/03/08/nx-s1-5741779/openai-resigns-ai-pentagon-guardrails-military). Kalinowski said that safeguards against domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons were not adequately defined before the deal was announced, calling her departure a ma...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 747 words. Clear separation of facts, context, unknowns, and analysis. Good use of direct quotes.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources verified: NPR (primary reporting, Kalinowski quotes, OpenAI response), Fortune (background on Kalinowski's career at Meta/Orion, Anthropic-Pentagon context), TechCrunch (departure confirmation, March 7 timeline), Engadget (OpenAI response, no replacement plans, Altman 'opportunistic' acknowledgment, 'rushed without guardrails' quote). All accessible and correctly cited.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims verified against sources: Kalinowski resigned March 7 (confirmed Fortune/TechCrunch), led robotics/hardware since November 2024 (confirmed Fortune), previous role at Meta on Orion AR glasses (confirmed Fortune), direct quotes about surveillance/lethal autonomy (confirmed NPR), 'rushed without the guardrails defined' (confirmed Engadget), OpenAI's red lines statement (confirmed NPR/Engadget), no plans to replace her (confirmed Engadget), Altman acknowledged 'opportunistic' perception (confirmed Fortune/Engadget). The specific date of February 27 for the Pentagon deal signing is stated but not explicitly confirmed in the fetched sources — minor concern. ChatGPT 295% uninstall figure matches existing archive article.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Presents both Kalinowski's objections and OpenAI's response fairly. The analysis section draws reasonable conclusions without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: Existing article (March 4) covers the consumer backlash/uninstall angle. This article covers Kalinowski's March 7 resignation — a genuinely new development in the developing story, with different sources and focus. Not a duplicate.
concerns: The February 27 date for the Pentagon deal signing is not explicitly confirmed in the fetched source content — it may be accurate but could not be cross-referenced from the four cited sources.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong news submission covering a significant new development in the OpenAI-Pentagon story. Well-sourced with proper attribution. Ready for publication.
